nodes.label: Unattended overnight execution
nodes.description: The pipeline must run from approximately 2am to completion without any human interaction.

nodes.label: Full price history per product
nodes.description: Every price observation must be persisted with a timestamp so the complete history is queryable over time.

nodes.label: Alert on price drop below threshold
nodes.description: When a tracked product's price falls below a user-configured threshold, an alert must be triggered automatically.

nodes.label: Cross-run deduplication
nodes.description: Scraping the same product in the same run must not produce duplicate records; dedup key is (product_id, run_id).

nodes.label: Auth-protected platform support
nodes.description: The scraper must handle Amazon and Walmart, which require an authenticated session via cookie replay.

nodes.label: Bot-detection resilience
nodes.description: The scraper must apply stealth measures sufficient to avoid trivial bot-detection on all three target platforms.

nodes.label: Per-product error isolation
nodes.description: A failure to scrape one product must not abort the pipeline; the error is logged and the run continues.

nodes.label: Offline scraping tests
nodes.description: Scraping and extraction logic must be testable against saved HTML fixtures without making live HTTP requests.

nodes.label: Python 3.11+ runtime
nodes.description: All pipeline code is written in Python 3.11+ running on Ubuntu 22.04.
nodes.rationale: User is most comfortable with Python; no competing requirement justifies a different language.

nodes.label: Playwright for browser automation
nodes.description: Use Playwright (Python bindings) with playwright-stealth for all scraping, including auth-protected sites.
nodes.rationale: Better async support, network interception API for fixture injection, and first-class stealth plugin compared to Selenium.

nodes.label: SQLite for persistence
nodes.description: Use SQLite (WAL mode). Schema: products, price_history (keyed by product_id + run_id), runs, thresholds tables.
nodes.rationale: Zero infrastructure overhead for a personal 50-product use case on a VPS.

nodes.label: Gmail SMTP for alerts
nodes.description: Send alert emails via smtplib using a Gmail app password stored in .env. Always send a run-summary email on completion.
nodes.rationale: User already has a Gmail account and wants zero additional cost. Run-summary email added for observability.

nodes.label: APScheduler for overnight scheduling
nodes.description: Use APScheduler BackgroundScheduler with a cron trigger to fire the pipeline at 2am daily.
nodes.rationale: Python-native scheduling keeps error handling within the same process.

nodes.label: Cookie-file authentication
nodes.description: User exports browser cookies to Netscape-format files; Playwright loads them at session launch for Amazon and Walmart. Expiry is detected and triggers a warning email.
nodes.rationale: Avoids storing plaintext credentials. Session is valid for 24-48h.

nodes.label: HTML fixture-based offline tests
nodes.description: Save real product HTML pages in tests/fixtures/; patch Playwright page routing to serve fixture content during tests.
nodes.rationale: Satisfies req_offline_testing while keeping tests deterministic and fast.

nodes.label: Product URLs remain stable
nodes.description: Assumes that product page URLs do not change or redirect unexpectedly between runs.

nodes.label: Price is present in rendered DOM
nodes.description: Assumes that after JavaScript rendering, the product price appears as text in the DOM reachable by a CSS selector.

nodes.label: Exported cookies stay valid 24-48h
nodes.description: Assumes that cookies exported from a logged-in browser session remain valid for at least one pipeline cycle.

nodes.label: Bot-detection rules change slowly
nodes.description: Assumes that fingerprinting logic on Amazon, eBay, and Walmart does not change more frequently than once per week.

nodes.label: Gmail SMTP available at 2am
nodes.description: Assumes Gmail SMTP is reachable from the VPS at the scheduled run time and that the app password does not expire without notice.

nodes.label: Bot detection breaks scraping
nodes.description: Amazon or Walmart may fingerprint the headless browser and return a CAPTCHA page or empty product data.

nodes.label: CSS selector breakage on site redesign
nodes.description: A site redesign can silently break price extraction, returning None rather than raising an exception.

nodes.label: CAPTCHA blocks unattended session
nodes.description: A CAPTCHA served mid-session has no automated bypass; the run stalls or skips the affected product.

nodes.label: Cookie expiry mid-run
nodes.description: If an Amazon or Walmart session cookie expires during the run, the scraper lands on a login wall and may silently record wrong data.

nodes.label: SMTP alert silently dropped
nodes.description: If the SMTP call fails (Gmail outage, wrong app password, blocked port), the alert is lost with no retry mechanism.

nodes.label: Pipeline run exceeds overnight window
nodes.description: With 50 products and 1-5s random delays, the run can take 30-60 minutes. A run-summary email mitigates the observability concern.

nodes.label: Platform scraper
nodes.description: Playwright-based scraper with one adapter per platform. Handles cookie loading, stealth, random delays, session-expiry detection, and price extraction.

nodes.label: SQLite database layer
nodes.description: Manages schema creation, upsert of price records with dedup by (product_id, run_id), threshold storage, and query helpers.

nodes.label: Threshold checker
nodes.description: After each successful scrape, queries the database for the latest price and compares against the configured threshold. Emits an AlertContract if crossed.

nodes.label: Email alert sender
nodes.description: SMTP-based alert module. Handles price-drop, session-expiry, and run-failure templates. Always sends a run-summary on completion.

nodes.label: Pipeline scheduler
nodes.description: APScheduler wrapper that sets up the 2am cron trigger, wraps the pipeline entry point, and sends a summary email on completion or failure.

nodes.label: PriceRecord data contract
nodes.description: Canonical data structure exchanged between the scraper, database, and threshold checker.
nodes.contract: { product_id: str, url: str, platform: str, price: float | None, currency: str, scraped_at: str (ISO8601), status: 'ok' | 'error' | 'session_expired', run_id: str }

nodes.label: AlertContract data contract
nodes.description: Data structure consumed by the alert component for all alert types.
nodes.contract: { product_id: str, product_name: str, current_price: float, threshold_price: float, platform: str, product_url: str, alert_type: 'price_drop' | 'session_expired' | 'run_failure' }

nodes.label: Scraper fixture tests
nodes.description: Unit tests for each platform adapter using saved HTML fixture files. Patches Playwright page routing. Validates PriceRecord shape and price value.

nodes.label: Deduplication integration test
nodes.description: Integration test using an in-memory SQLite database. Inserts two PriceRecords with identical (product_id, run_id) and asserts only one row is persisted.

nodes.label: Alert trigger unit test
nodes.description: Unit test that mocks smtplib.SMTP and asserts that the alert sender is called with the correct AlertContract when a price drops below threshold.
